nodes.label: Non-production Security Boundary
nodes.rationale: The reference implementation is not approved for real funds, public networks, production signers, or compliance reliance.

nodes.label: Pending Outbox Is Not Execution
nodes.rationale: A pending local outbox record proves durable acceptance only, not processing or any external effect.

nodes.label: ADR 0001 Maven Reactor and Module Boundaries
nodes.norm_label: adr 0001 maven reactor and module boundaries

nodes.label: No Speculative Adapter Modules
nodes.rationale: Adapter modules are created only when a vertical slice uses and tests them, avoiding speculative structure.

nodes.norm_label: adr 0004 postgresql jdbc flyway atomic outbox

nodes.label: Outbox Worker Deferred to Phase 3B
nodes.rationale: A durable pending outbox record preserves atomic intent while polling, leasing, publication, retry, and recovery remain a later phase.

nodes.label: Accepted ADR Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: Accepted records govern current decisions; supersession requires a new ADR so historical rationale is not rewritten.

nodes.label: Runtime Configuration and Static Gate Evidence
nodes.norm_label: runtime configuration and static gate evidence

nodes.label: Phase 3B Processing Deferral
nodes.rationale: Durable acceptance deliberately records one pending outbox event without polling, publication, processing, signing, chain submission, or another external effect; those recovery mechanics require their own Phase 3B gate.

nodes.label: Non-Production Safety Boundary
nodes.rationale: The repository demonstrates production-oriented controls without authorizing real funds, credentials, public networks, or production signing authority.

nodes.label: Java and Spring Regulated Control Plane
nodes.norm_label: java and spring regulated control plane

nodes.label: Durable Internal Business Truth
nodes.rationale: Signatures, transaction hashes, RPC responses, receipts, and commitments are evidence rather than complete financial truth.

nodes.label: Blockchain, Legal, Customer, and Accounting Finalities
nodes.norm_label: blockchain, legal, customer, and accounting finalities

nodes.label: Append-Only Compensation
nodes.source_location: ## 19. Error taxonomy, retry safety, and compensation
nodes.rationale: Confirmed external effects remain in history; compensation is a separately authorized durable operation rather than destructive rollback.

nodes.label: Asynchronous Saga with Narrow Transactions
nodes.rationale: Bank and chain effects cannot share a distributed transaction, so durable identities, local commits, inquiry, and append-only compensation coordinate progress.

nodes.label: Separate Ethereum and Solana Completion Claims
nodes.rationale: Ethereum and Solana preserve different native semantics and therefore require separately evidenced end-to-end completion claims.
nodes.norm_label: separate ethereum and solana completion claims

nodes.label: Documentation-Only Scope
nodes.rationale: The action improves reviewer-facing specifications without changing executable capability, dependencies, schemas, APIs, signers, adapters, contracts, programs, or runtime configuration.

nodes.label: Zelle Public Case Study Boundary
nodes.rationale: Reviewer-facing material must not imply affiliation, endorsement, confidential architecture, selected vendors, or an announced Zelle or Early Warning Services implementation plan.

nodes.label: Asynchronous Saga with Narrow Transactions
nodes.rationale: Confirmed external effects cannot be destructively rolled back, so compensation is a new authorized operation with separate identity and evidence.

nodes.label: Ethereum-First Chain Sequence
nodes.rationale: Ethereum and Solana remain separate local demonstrations and implementation slices so native chain semantics are validated independently; Ethereum remains first.
